note_en: Arena Autopilot — two simulated $10,000 ledgers (Model A: intraday double-window swing; Model B: end-of-day deep review) trading a fixed universe (public/arena-universe.json) under code-enforced risk rules (public/../src/lib/arenaRules.js — the model proposes, the rules engine settles, never the other way around). Not investment advice.
note_zh: Arena Autopilot——两本各 $10,000 的模拟账本（Model A：日内双窗口 swing；Model B：盘后深度复盘），在固定交易域内（public/arena-universe.json）按代码强制的风控规则运作（src/lib/arenaRules.js——模型只提案，规则引擎收单，永远不是反过来）。非投资建议。

review.zh: 半导体今日再度普跌：SNDK/WDC因中国长鑫存储(CXMT)86亿美元DRAM IPO引发产能过剩担忧续跌7%，AI服务器硬件(Dell -14%、HPE -8%、SMCI -5%)因资本开支/利润率担忧同步重挫，TSMC财报超预期但市场只关注资本开支成本。AMD已连续两日下跌(-3.75%、-3.26%)，累计浮亏约7.3%，且此轮下跌已从存储蔓延至更广泛的算力/服务器硬件板块，不再是单一行业噪音。吸取上次TSM、MU拖延止损导致亏损扩大的教训，本次主动卖出AMD锁定损失，而非等待反转。NVDA、CRM今日基本持平/小涨，维持不动。本次不新增仓位——大盘科技股(AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN)逆势上涨为资金避险轮动信号，追涨半导体反弹时机未到。
review.en: Broad chip de-risking day: memory names extended losses (SNDK/WDC -7%) on CXMT's $8.6B DRAM IPO stoking oversupply fears, while AI server hardware (Dell -14%, HPE -8%, SMCI -5%) sold off on capex/margin worries; TSMC beat but market ignored it. AMD is down two straight sessions (-3.75%, -3.26%), now ~-7.3% unrealized, and the rout has broadened beyond memory into compute/server hardware broadly. Learning from TSM and MU, where I held too long into weakness before eventually selling at a loss, I'm proactively trimming AMD here instead of waiting for a reversal. Holding NVDA and CRM (both flat-to-up today). No new buys — megacap tech's relative strength (AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN green) looks like a safety rotation, not a signal to chase semis into this drawdown.

review.zh: 周五非周二/周四开仓窗口，仅考虑减仓。MSFT现价384.93(-0.5%)、GOOGL现价359.51(-1.9%)，均未触及风控阈值。今日存储芯片板块因CXMT存储IPO过剩担忧及台积电资本开支拖累估值普跌，但集中在核心AI硬件股，Book B未持有。Alphabet资本开支上调至1800-1900亿美元、7月22日财报临近，长线逻辑未变。维持低换手率纪律，本次不动仓。
review.en: Friday is not a Tue/Thu buy window, so only sells were considered; neither MSFT (-0.5% vs cost) nor GOOGL (-1.9%) breaches a risk threshold, and today's memory-chip selloff (CXMT DRAM IPO fears, TSMC capex-driven de-rating) is concentrated in core-ai-hardware names Book B doesn't hold. Alphabet's capex raise to $180-190B and July 22 earnings keep the long thesis intact. Holding both, no orders this run — same low-turnover discipline as last review.
